description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a brownish crustacean, likely a crayfish or lobster, featuring a segmented body with a prominent cephalothorax and elongated abdomen. The creature has two large, robust claws (chelae) at the front, which are slightly open and display detailed articulation. Its body is supported by multiple slender walking legs, and the tail fans out at the rear, showing the typical structure of aquatic crustaceans. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, with a matte to semi-glossy finish, and the coloration ranges from dark brown to lighter, reddish-brown hues. Antennae are visible extending from the head, and the eyes are positioned on short stalks. The overall form is naturalistic, with anatomical accuracy in the segmentation and appendages.
description: A brown, segmented crustacean with large claws, multiple legs, and a fan-shaped tail, featuring realistic surface detail and coloration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the segmentation of the tail and legs.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the claws, the segmentation of the back, and the spread of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the other profile of the claws, legs, and body segmentation.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the two large claws, the head, and the arrangement of the legs beneath the body.
